zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant於1224年6月14日出生在法國洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯時，她的父親Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant35歲，母親Maria von Staufen23歲。她於1237年6月14日與Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois在法國皮卡迪奧易可皮艾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1288年9月29日在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯爾坎普享年64歲，埋葬在法國阿托斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant于1224年6月14日出生在法国洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯时，她的父亲Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant35岁，母亲Maria von Staufen23岁。她于1237年6月14日与Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois在法国皮卡迪奧易可皮艾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1288年9月29日在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯爾坎普享年64岁，埋葬在法国阿托斯。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: artois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the French province of Artois, from Anglo-Norman French Arteis (from Latin Atrebates, the name of the local Gaulish tribe).
French: from Old French artis ‘woodworm’, Old Occitan arta ‘moth’, possibly applied as a nickname for someone suffering from a wasting disease, perhaps leprosy.

variants.name: Artist
meanings: English: alteration, by folk etymology, of   Artis  .

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant was born on 14 June 1224, in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France, her father, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, was 35 and her mother, Maria von Staufen, was 23. She married Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois on 14 June 1237, in Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 29 September 1288, in Cercamp, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 64, and was buried in Artois, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant est née le 14 juin 1224, à Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France, son père, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Maria von Staufen, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois le 14 juin 1237, à Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 septembre 1288, à Cercamp, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Artois, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant am 14. Juni 1224 in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant 35 und ihre Mutter Maria von Staufen 23. Sie hat am 14. Juni 1237 in Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, Frankreich Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. September 1288 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Cercamp, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich und wurde in Artois, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant è nata il il 14 giugno 1224 nel Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, sua padre, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Maria von Staufen, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois il 14 giugno 1237, nel Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 29 settembre 1288, nel Cercamp, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Artois, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda, countess of artois of Brabantが1224年6月14日にフランスロレィヌメウゼブラバント-スル-メウゼで生まれた時，父Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabantは35歳，母Maria von Staufenは23歳であった。1237年6月14日にフランスピカーディオイゼコンパeグンでRobert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artoisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1288年9月29日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイスセルカンップで64歳で死去し，フランスアルトイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant(이)가 1224년 6월 14일 프랑스 로레이인 우스 브라반트-수-우스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maria von Staufen(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1237년 6월 14일 프랑스 피카디 이스 콤피e그니에서 Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1288년 9월 29일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 세르캠프에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 아토이스에 묻혔다.

mn.14 June 1224–29 September 1288: 1224 оны Зургаадугаар сарын 14–1288 оны Есдүгээр сарын 29

pt.14 June 1224–29 September 1288: 14 de junho de 1224–29 de setembro de 1288
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant nasceu em 14 de junho de 1224, em Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, França, seu pai, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Maria von Staufen, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois em 14 de junho de 1237, em Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de setembro de 1288, em Cercamp, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Artois, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant родилась 14 июня 1224 г. в местности брабант-сур-меус, меус, лорреаин, Франция, её отцу по имени Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria von Staufen, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois 14 июня 1237 г. в местности компиeгн, оис, пикарди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 29 сентября 1288 г. в местности киркамп, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности артоис, Франция.

es.14 June 1224–29 September 1288: 14 de junio de 1224–29 de septiembre de 1288
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant nació el 14 de junio de 1224, en Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, su papá, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Maria von Staufen, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois el 14 de junio de 1237, en Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 29 de septiembre de 1288, en Cercamp, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Artois, Francia.
